title: $MACRO_FIRST
text: $MACRO_FIRST Cause macros to be processed before the rest of the keyboard input queue is handled. $MACRO_FIRST = 1 | 0 Defaults Assignment file: application assignment files Section: [SETTINGS] Default value: 0 Description The default behavior of Uniface is to process all other keys in the keyboard input queue until the queue is empty, and then to process any macros. Setting $MACRO_FIRST to 1 causes macros to be processed before the rest of the keyboard input queue is handled. Related Links Macro Definitions The translation table compiler supports up to ten macros, ^M0 through ^M9, for commonly-used combinations. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
